old_claim.note: Expert systems encode knowledge bases and inference rules in software; software engineering was previously treated as a prerequisite for this early AI software class.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains from Symbolic AI & Expert Systems to software_engineering. The scoped node is the method lineage of symbolic reasoning and expertise encoding, not a broad software-engineering process prerequisite.

source_shape.source_locator: The DENDRAL history source presents expert-system practice in early AI contexts and does not model a direct dependency on modern software engineering process discipline.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source profiles DENDRAL as a 1960s rule-based expert system, with dependency on symbolic AI lineage and knowledge-encoding techniques.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports removing the direct software-engineering edge because it separates conceptual lineage from later development methodology, which is an implicit deployment context rather than a defining prerequisite.

ontology_before: Symbolic AI & Expert Systems directly depended on software_engineering, and earlier an additional proposed replacement to relational DBMS was considered.
ontology_after: The node now keeps only explicit lineage-level dependencies and no direct software-engineering edge.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: software_engineering is no longer a direct prerequisite. Preserved: symbolic knowledge representation and AI antecedent nodes remain in place.
would_reject_if: A later source showed that all expert systems in this scope are contingent on software engineering doctrine as a hard prerequisite.
short_reason: This was implementation context, not a required conceptual dependency.
